findings.title: M-DCR-003 delivery target contradicts PS-DCR-006 SLA
findings.detail: M-DCR-003 states a target of 'Within 4-6 business days of the order', but PS-DCR-006 carries an SLA of 'Card delivered within 5-7 business days of the order' and M-DCR-003's own provenance records the source as '5-7 business days'. The metric and the step disagree on the committed delivery window.
findings.resolutionNote: PS-DCR-006 SLA aligned to the 4-6 business day delivery window — the figure the SLA update memo established and conflict-resolution already applied to M-DCR-003.

findings.title: CP-DCR-002 execution and owner contradict PS-DCR-003 description
findings.detail: CP-DCR-002 marks the card block as execution: AUTOMATED with owner: Card Operations, but PS-DCR-003 states 'The agent places an immediate block' and lists owner: Contact Centre Agent. The two elements disagree on both who initiates the block and whether it is automated or manual.
findings.resolutionNote: CP-DCR-002 corrected to execution MANUAL, owner Contact Centre — the immediate block is placed by the Contact Centre Agent (PS-DCR-003), not an automated system; the DTP control table's 'automated' label was loose.

findings.title: PP-DCR-004 and CG-DCR-001 describe the same delivery-visibility gap but are not linked
findings.detail: PP-DCR-004 (no delivery tracking once the card leaves for the bureau, affects PS-DCR-006) and CG-DCR-001 (no control confirms the replacement card reached the customer) both document the same untracked-delivery problem from different perspectives. Neither element references the other, making the gap appear twice in the wiki without a cross-link.
findings.resolutionNote: By design: PP-DCR-004 (staff/process pain) and CG-DCR-001 (control gap) document the same delivery-visibility issue through two perspectives the content model keeps as separate element types; the schema has no pain-point-to-compliance-gap relation. The client-lens view FP-DCR-003 is linked to PP-DCR-004 via painPoint.

findings.title: PP-DCR-001 describes a control weakness at intake with no corresponding compliance-gap element
findings.detail: PP-DCR-001 documents that manual re-keying of the reported reason at PS-DCR-001 causes mis-routing — a damaged card sent through the fraud check, or a stolen card that skips it. This is a control weakness at the intake step, but no CG-DCR element captures it. CG-DCR-001 covers only the delivery-confirmation gap.
findings.resolutionNote: Created CG-DCR-003 — the intake step has no control over the accuracy of the reported reason, which routes the fraud check; the control weakness PP-DCR-001 describes is now captured as a compliance-gap.

findings.title: Innovation-ideas section is empty despite four documented pain points
findings.detail: PP-DCR-001 through PP-DCR-004 are all documented, including the P1/HIGH pain point PP-DCR-002 (knowledge-based identity verification slow and failing often). The innovation-ideas section contains no elements at all. Is the innovation section intentionally deferred to a later phase, or should ideas addressing at least the highest-priority pain points be captured now?
findings.resolutionNote: Innovation is intentionally deferred — the Innovation area is documented in its own phase via source-innovation and innovation-analyst, after the As-Is, Risk and CX perspectives. The four pain points are recorded inputs to that later pass.

findings.title: SYS-DCR-002 and SYS-DCR-003 have no documented integration with SYS-DCR-001
findings.detail: Steps PS-DCR-001 and PS-DCR-002 span SYS-DCR-002 (Contact Centre CRM) and SYS-DCR-003 (Mobile Banking App), while PS-DCR-003 onward operates in SYS-DCR-001 (CMS). Neither intake system carries an 'integrates' link to SYS-DCR-001. Is the hand-off between intake channels and the CMS handled by a direct system integration that should be documented, or is it a manual step?
findings.resolutionNote: Deferred to the IT Architect — the Integrations section is not yet documented; whether the intake-to-CMS hand-off is a system integration or a manual step is logged for the it-architect session.
